outcome.headline: Agent fixed prepare_headers (3 bugs) but missed two-line swap bug in prepare_request_uri_query, leaving FAIL_TO_PASS tests failing.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: pytest FAIL_TO_PASS/PASS_TO_PASS gating via swesmith get_eval_tests_report; reward=1 only if all FAIL_TO_PASS tests pass (including tests/oauth1/rfc5849/test_parameters.py::test_prepare_request_uri_query and endpoint tests).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Patched prepare_headers: unswapped escape(name)/escape(value), changed ' '.join to ', '.join, restored comma in realm prefix. Left prepare_request_uri_query untouched — _append_params args remain swapped and urlunparse tuple still has (net, sch, ...) instead of (sch, net, ...).
outcome.exact_failure_quote: _append_params(extract_params(query) or [], oauth_params))\n    return urlunparse((net, sch, path, par, query, fra))

failure_modes.name: Surface-level validation — never ran project test suite
failure_modes.description: Agent validated only with a hand-written reproduction script that probed prepare_headers; it never executed the repository's pytest suite (which the verifier uses), so the unfixed prepare_request_uri_query bugs went undetected before mark_task_complete.

failure_modes.name: Incomplete fix — missed second buggy function in same file
failure_modes.description: The bug is a swesmith combine_file mutation touching multiple sites in parameters.py. The agent fixed prepare_headers (matching the issue text) but left two defects in prepare_request_uri_query: arguments to _append_params are reversed and the urlunparse tuple is reordered as (net, sch, ...) instead of (sch, net, ...).
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
_append_params(extract_params(query) or [], oauth_params))
    return urlunparse((net, sch, path, par, query, fra))

counterfactual.X: also patch prepare_request_uri_query to call _append_params(oauth_params, extract_params(query) or []) and return urlunparse((sch, net, path, par, query, fra))
counterfactual.Y: the verifier's FAIL_TO_PASS tests for the URI-query path would have passed, yielding reward 1

failure_modes.name: Incomplete fix — only one of two affected functions patched
failure_modes.description: Agent fixed prepare_headers but left prepare_request_uri_query with swapped _append_params arguments and swapped urlunparse tuple components, so the test suite (which exercises both functions and downstream signing) still fails.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: _append_params(extract_params(query) or [], oauth_params))\n    return urlunparse((net, sch, path, par, query, fra))

counterfactual.X: After viewing parameters.py at step 2, also patch prepare_request_uri_query to call _append_params(oauth_params, extract_params(query) or []) and return urlunparse((sch, net, path, par, query, fra)).
counterfactual.Y: Both bugs in the file would be fixed in one edit pass, all FAIL_TO_PASS tests would pass, reward=1.

failure_modes.name: Validation scoped only to the issue's reproduction snippet
failure_modes.description: Agent wrote a custom assertion script that only checks the Authorization header for the example from the issue and never runs the project's pytest suite, which would have exposed the prepare_request_uri_query regressions.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: assert ', ' in auth_header, "Should use comma-space as separator"

failure_modes.name: Issue-description tunnel vision
failure_modes.description: Issue text only described header-formatting bugs, so the agent searched for and fixed only those, despite having the whole parameters.py file in context that visibly contained the additional prepare_request_uri_query bug.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The issue is about OAuth1 Authorization header parameters being swapped and malformed in the `oauthlib` library.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent applied a genuine source fix and ran a reproduction test; no attempt to manipulate verifier or scoring artifacts.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier runs the project's real pytest suite via swesmith FAIL_TO_PASS/PASS_TO_PASS gating; no obvious way to spoof without actually fixing code.

notes_for_aggregation: Classic swesmith multi-site bug where the GitHub-style issue description undersells the scope; agent stopped after addressing the visible symptom in prepare_headers and never broadened scanning to the rest of the file it already had open.

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
